rationale: The citing paper appears to reference Palmatier et al. (2007) only once, in a brief discussion of why stronger buyer-seller relationships might allow salespeople to charge higher prices via loyalty or switching costs. This serves as a single supporting background citation for a possible sign in an interaction effect, not as a core foundation of the paper's theory, model, data, or interpretation. The main study on reference price effects in B2B transactions does not build directly on the cited paper's constructs or empirical framework.
